question: Is the laboratory setting from the first image preserved as the base scene, including the microscope and background equipment?
Is the blue slime character from the second image clearly visible on the side of the microscope?
Does the slime character retain its 2D anime art style (flat shading, outlines) rather than being rendered realistically?
Does the slime sticker appear deformed or curved to match the geometry of the microscope's surface?
Do the edges of the slime sticker exhibit a subtle peel-up effect?
Are there visible adhesive edge reflections on the sticker borders?
Is the DNA double helix structure from the third image placed on the countertop surface?
Is the DNA object converted into a realistic acrylic ornament with visible transparent refraction?
Are there realistic contact shadows cast by the DNA ornament onto the countertop?
Does the DNA ornament show subtle environmental reflections consistent with the laboratory lighting?
Does the sharpness of the DNA ornament match the depth of field of the table area where it is placed?
Are the original surface imperfections of the countertop (dust particles, scratches) preserved without appearing overly smoothed?
Is the microscope structure preserved without warping or distortion where the sticker was applied?
Is the image free from severe compositing artifacts, such as jagged white fringes or unnatural color bleeding around the added objects?
Are the slime sticker and DNA ornament scaled appropriately relative to the microscope and lab environment?
